tools.name: shape_post
tools.description: Turn rough notes about what the user did (shipped a feature, fixed a bug, hit a number) into 2-3 finished social-post angles in the user's voice. Grounded only in the notes - never invents facts. Uses one AI generation from the user's monthly quota.

tools.name: create_draft
tools.description: Save a post as a DRAFT in the user's posthell queue. It is never published by this tool - the user approves it in the dashboard. Optionally propose a publish time (pre-fills their scheduler) and limit which platforms it targets (default: every connected account).

tools.name: list_posts
tools.description: Read the user's recent posts and drafts with per-platform status. Published posts include engagement metrics (likes, comments, shares, engagementRate - impressions on plans with full analytics) once analytics have synced. Use it to avoid duplicating a topic AND to learn which topics performed before

tools.name: get_growth
tools.description: The user's follower counts and 7-day growth per connected network, plus which network is growing fastest. Use this to decide where a post matters most or to report progress.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_posts, get_growth · Public sink: shape_post, create_draft, list_posts, get_growth.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_posts, get_growth · Public sink: shape_post, create_draft, list_posts, get_growth

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
